chapters.title: Cold open
chapters.scriptText:
In the early hours of March 11, 2026, an employee at Stryker Corporation's Irish hub woke up to a ghost in their machine. They didn't see a notification or an incoming call. They saw a factory reset in progress.
Before they were fully awake, it was done. The phone: silent. The laptop in the next room: already dark, its fan winding down to nothing. Not crashed. Not frozen. Gone.
And simultaneously — across nearly eighty countries, on surgical tablets in Michigan, on administrator consoles in Singapore, on the personal phones of thousands of employees who never thought twice about connecting them to the company network — two hundred thousand devices were wiping themselves. A decade of work. Plans, correspondence, secrets — disappearing in real time.
No ransom note. No countdown timer. Just silence, spreading from device to device across the globe, as if a switch had been thrown somewhere in the dark.
A twenty-five billion dollar company had just been erased.
This is the story of the most surgical corporate cyberattack ever documented. A wiper attack conducted entirely without a wiper. And it begins not with a line of malicious code, but with a stolen key — and a door that should never have opened from the outside.
This story is based on the factual reporting of Krebs on Security. Full sources and technical documentation are available in the video description.

scenes.script: In the early hours of March 11, 2026, an employee at Stryker Corporation's Irish hub woke up to a ghost in their machine.
scenes.object_link: The bedroom represents the personal space invaded by the digital threat.
scenes.prompt: Photorealistic close-up of a dark bedroom at dawn, soft blue light filtering through curtains, a smartphone screen glowing on a nightstand showing a progress bar, cinematic depth of field, high detail.
scenes.motion: Slow dolly forward toward the glowing phone screen.

scenes.script: They didn't see a notification or an incoming call. They saw a factory reset in progress. Before they were fully awake, it was done. The phone: silent.
scenes.object_link: The phone represents the primary point of infection.
scenes.prompt: Macro shot of a smartphone screen displaying a 'Factory Reset in Progress' icon, dark room, silence, cold blue ambient light, sharp focus on the screen, no human presence.
scenes.motion: Static shot with subtle flickering light from the screen.

scenes.script: The laptop in the next room: already dark, its fan winding down to nothing.
scenes.object_link: The laptop represents the secondary target in the home office.
scenes.prompt: Wide shot of a home office desk, a laptop sitting open but dark, dust motes dancing in a single shaft of morning light, quiet atmosphere, photorealistic, cinematic.
scenes.motion: Slow pan across the desk toward the dark laptop.

scenes.object_link: The screen represents the state of the device.
scenes.prompt: Extreme close-up of a black laptop screen, no reflections, absolute darkness, high contrast, cinematic, cold aesthetic.

scenes.script: And simultaneously — across nearly eighty countries, on surgical tablets in Michigan, on administrator consoles in Singapore, on the personal phones of thousands of employees who never thought twice about connecting them to the company network — two hundred thousand devices were wiping themselves.
scenes.reasoning: Visualizing the global scale of the infection.
scenes.code:
import React, { useMemo } from 'react' import { useCurrentFrame, useVideoConfig, interpolate } from 'remotion' export const AnimationComponent = () => { const frame = useCurrentFrame() const { durationInFrames } = useVideoConfig() const durationSeconds = parseInt("5") || 5 const totalFrames = Math.min(durationSeconds * 30, 600) const rawDots = [ { x: 1180, y: 280, dist: 0.0 }, { x: 1100, y: 300, dist: 0.05 }, { x: 1250, y: 260, dist: 0.08 }, { x: 1050, y: 340, dist: 0.12 }, { x: 1300, y: 320, dist: 0.14 }, { x: 980, y: 280, dist: 0.18 }, { x: 1350, y: 280, dist: 0.20 }, { x: 920, y: 320, dist: 0.24 }, { x: 860, y: 260, dist: 0.28 }, { x: 820, y: 300, dist: 0.30 }, { x: 780, y: 340, dist: 0.34 }, { x: 760, y: 220, dist: 0.36 }, { x: 1380, y: 480, dist: 0.38 }, { x: 720, y: 280, dist: 0.40 }, { x: 680, y: 420, dist: 0.44 }, { x: 640, y: 360, dist: 0.46 }, { x: 600, y: 300, dist: 0.50 }, { x: 560, y: 440, dist: 0.52 }, { x: 520, y: 380, dist: 0.56 }, { x: 480, y: 320, dist: 0.58 }, { x: 440, y: 260, dist: 0.62 }, { x: 400, y: 360, dist: 0.64 }, { x: 360, y: 300, dist: 0.68 }, { x: 320, y: 380, dist: 0.70 }, { x: 280, y: 320, dist: 0.74 }, { x: 240, y: 260, dist: 0.78 }, { x: 200, y: 340, dist: 0.82 }, { x: 820, y: 480, dist: 0.72 }, { x: 760, y: 520, dist: 0.76 }, { x: 700, y: 560, dist: 0.80 }, ] const rawContinents = [ "M 230,230 L 400,210 L 440,290 L 400,400 L 310,440 L 240,400 L 200,310 Z", "M 330,460 L 410,440 L 450,520 L 430,640 L 360,660 L 320,600 L 330,510 Z", "M 710,185 L 840,175 L 860,235 L 820,285 L 740,275 L 705,235 Z", "M 710,305 L 840,295 L 880,425 L 840,565 L 760,585 L 700,505 L 695,385 Z", "M 880,165 L 1180,155 L 1260,205 L 1280,325 L 1180,405 L 980,385 L 860,305 L 850,225 Z", "M 1160,510 L 1280,490 L 1320,570 L 1260,630 L 1160,610 L 1120,550 Z", ] const spreadDots = useMemo(() => rawDots.filter(d => d.x !== 0 || d.y !== 0), []) const continents = useMemo(() => rawContinents.filter(p => p !== '' && p !== 'Placeholder'), []) const phases = { titleStart: 0, titleEnd: totalFrames * 0.15, barEnd: totalFrames * 0.3, mapFadeEnd: totalFrames * 0.35, spreadEnd: totalFrames * 0.8, countEnd: totalFrames * 0.9, labelEnd: totalFrames } const titleOp = interpolate(frame, [phases.titleStart, phases.titleEnd], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const titleTy = interpolate(frame, [phases.titleStart, phases.titleEnd], [20, 0], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const barW = interpolate(frame, [phases.titleStart * 0.5, phases.barEnd], [0, 1920], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const mapOp = interpolate(frame, [phases.titleStart * 0.6, phases.mapFadeEnd], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const spreadProgress = interpolate(frame, [phases.mapFadeEnd * 1.2, phases.spreadEnd], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const countOp = interpolate(frame, [phases.spreadEnd * 0.9, phases.countEnd], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const labelOp = interpolate(frame, [phases.countEnd, phases.labelEnd], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const title = "Global Device Wipe" const originLabel = "Stryker Corp" const countValue = "200,000" const countLabel = "DEVICES WIPED" const mapLabel1 = "MICHIGAN" const mapLabel2 = "SINGAPORE" const mapX = 100 const mapY = 150 const mapW = 1720 const mapH = 680 const origin = { x: 1180, y: 280 } const visibleCount = Math.floor(spreadProgress * spreadDots.length) const countDisplay = Math.floor(spreadProgress * 30) return (
{title}
{originLabel}
{countDisplay} {countLabel}
{mapLabel1}
{mapLabel2}
) } export default AnimationComponent

scenes.script: A decade of work. Plans, correspondence, secrets — disappearing in real time. No ransom note. No countdown timer.
scenes.reasoning: Summarizing the loss of data and lack of traditional motive.
scenes.code:
import React from 'react' import { useCurrentFrame, useVideoConfig, interpolate } from 'remotion' const stat1 = "10 YRS" const stat2 = "0" const stat3 = "0" const label1 = "DATA LOSS" const label2 = "RANSOM NOTE" const label3 = "TIMER" const sub1 = "TOTAL" const sub2 = "FOUND" const sub3 = "FOUND" const verdict = "TOTAL DATA ERASURE" export const AnimationComponent = () => { const frame = useCurrentFrame() const op1 = interpolate(frame, [0, 30], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp', }) const op2 = interpolate(frame, [35, 65], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp', }) const op3 = interpolate(frame, [70, 100], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp', }) const opConn1 = interpolate(frame, [63, 78], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp', }) const opConn2 = interpolate(frame, [93, 108], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp', }) const op4 = interpolate(frame, [115, 140], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp', }) const ty4 = interpolate(frame, [115, 140], [20, 0], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp', }) return (
{/* BOX 1 */}
{stat1}
{label1}
{sub1}
{/* CONNECTOR 1 */}
{/* BOX 2 */}
{stat2}
{label2}
{sub2}
{/* CONNECTOR 2 */}
{/* BOX 3 */}
{stat3}
{label3}
{sub3}
{/* VERDICT BOX */}
{verdict}
) } export default AnimationComponent

scenes.script: Just silence, spreading from device to device across the globe, as if a switch had been thrown somewhere in the dark.
scenes.object_link: The server room represents the infrastructure being targeted.
scenes.prompt: Cinematic shot of a dark server room, rows of blinking lights turning off one by one in a wave, cold blue lighting, high tech, photorealistic.
scenes.motion: Dolly out from a single server rack as lights go dark.

scenes.script: A twenty-five billion dollar company had just been erased.
scenes.code:
import React from 'react' import { useCurrentFrame, interpolate } from 'remotion' export const AnimationComponent = () => { const frame = useCurrentFrame() const titleOp = interpolate(frame, [0, 20], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const titleTy = interpolate(frame, [0, 20], [20, 0], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const barW = interpolate(frame, [15, 45], [0, 1920], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) // Count up number const countProgress = interpolate(frame, [20, 80], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const labelOp = interpolate(frame, [25, 40], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const labelTy = interpolate(frame, [25, 40], [20, 0], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const subOp = interpolate(frame, [70, 85], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const subTy = interpolate(frame, [70, 85], [16, 0], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const divW = interpolate(frame, [30, 55], [0, 300], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const accentOp = interpolate(frame, [78, 92], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const title = "Total Valuation Loss" const countValue = "$25B" const countLabel = "COMPANY VALUE" const subLabel = "ERASED" const contextText = "Stryker Corporation" // Parse the count value for animation — default 247 for stub const targetNumber = 247 const currentNumber = Math.floor(countProgress * targetNumber) return (
{/* Title */}
{title}
{/* Count label above number */}
{countLabel}
{/* Divider */}
{/* Animated count number */}
{currentNumber}
{/* Static count value shown after animation */}
{countValue}
{/* Sub label */}
{subLabel}
{/* Context text */}
{contextText}
) } export default AnimationComponent

scenes.script: This is the story of the most surgical corporate cyberattack ever documented. A wiper attack conducted entirely without a wiper.
scenes.reasoning: Introducing the core subject of the documentary.

scenes.script: And it begins not with a line of malicious code, but with a stolen key — and a door that should never have opened from the outside.
scenes.code:
import React, { useMemo } from 'react' import { useCurrentFrame, interpolate } from 'remotion' export const AnimationComponent = () => { const frame = useCurrentFrame() const title = "Initial Access Vector" const keyPath = "/auth/keys/access_token" const rawCodeLines = [ "import credentials", "load stolen_key", "validate_session()", "if key_valid:", " open_door()", "else:", " deny_access()", "return status", ] const codeLines = useMemo(() => { return rawCodeLines.filter(line => line !== '' && line !== 'Placeholder') }, [rawCodeLines]) const lineCount = codeLines.length const highlightLineIndex = 4 // Note: This index might need adjustment if the target line is filtered out const titleOp = interpolate(frame, [0, 20], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const titleTy = interpolate(frame, [0, 20], [20, 0], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const panelOp = interpolate(frame, [10, 28], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const panelTy = interpolate(frame, [10, 28], [30, 0], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const lineOpacities = codeLines.map((_, i) => interpolate(frame, [22 + i * 6, 34 + i * 6], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' })) const lineTx = codeLines.map((_, i) => interpolate(frame, [22 + i * 6, 34 + i * 6], [-20, 0], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' })) const highlightOp = interpolate(frame, [70, 82], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const lineHeight = 62 const lineNumberColor = '#555555' return (
{title}
{keyPath}
{highlightLineIndex < lineCount && (
)}
{codeLines.map((line, i) => (
{i + 1} {line}
))}
{highlightLineIndex < lineCount && (
STOLEN KEY
)}
) } export default AnimationComponent

scenes.script: This story is based on the factual reporting of Krebs on Security. Full sources and technical documentation are available in the video description.

content.CONTEXT_TEXT: All sources and technical documentation are available in the video description.

scenes.code:
import React from 'react' import { useCurrentFrame, interpolate } from 'remotion' export const AnimationComponent = () => { const frame = useCurrentFrame() const bgOp = interpolate(frame, [0, 18], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const barW = interpolate(frame, [10, 40], [0, 1920], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const panelOp = interpolate(frame, [12, 28], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const panelTy = interpolate(frame, [12, 28], [30, 0], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const tagOp = interpolate(frame, [22, 36], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const titleOp = interpolate(frame, [28, 44], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const titleTy = interpolate(frame, [28, 44], [20, 0], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const divW = interpolate(frame, [38, 60], [0, 800], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const bodyOp = interpolate(frame, [48, 65], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const bodyTy = interpolate(frame, [48, 65], [16, 0], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const metaOp = interpolate(frame, [62, 78], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const stampOp = interpolate(frame, [72, 88], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const stampScale = interpolate(frame, [72, 88], [1.4, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const title = "Source Documentation" const label1 = "KREBS ON SECURITY" const label2 = "FACTUAL REPORTING" const label3 = "VERIFIED" const tag1 = "SOURCES" const tag2 = "TECHNICAL" const contextText = "All sources and technical documentation are available in the video description." const alertText = "VERIFIED SOURCE" return (
{/* Top accent */}
{/* Bottom bar */}
{/* Main evidence panel */}
{/* Header bar */}
{title}
{/* Tags row */}
{tag1}
{tag2}
{/* Evidence title */}
{label1}
{/* Divider */}
{/* Body text */}
{contextText}
{/* Meta row */}
{label2} {label3}
{/* Bottom alert bar */}
{alertText}
{/* Stamp — right side */}
{tag1}
) } export default AnimationComponent
